title: The Output Gate
concept: The output gate decides what the next hidden state should be, filtering the updated cell state through a sigmoid function.

exercises.prompt: Which gate is responsible for determining the next hidden state by filtering the cell state?
exercises.explain: The output gate decides what information to release from the cell state to the next hidden state.

cards.title: What gets shared?
cards.body: An LSTM has stored a lot of long-term memories in its cell state. But how does it decide what to actually use and say next? That's where the Output Gate comes in—acting as a filter to make sure we only share what's useful right now!

cards.title: What is the Output Gate?
cards.body: The Output Gate is the final gate in an LSTM cell. It decides what information from the internal cell state is sent out as the hidden state. It uses a sigmoid function to create a filter (values between 0 and 1) and multiplies it by the squashed cell state to ensure only the relevant information gets passed along.

cards.title: The Security Guard Analogy
cards.body: Think of the LSTM cell as a secure archives room. The Cell State is a giant whiteboard inside the room holding all historical facts. The Output Gate is like a security guard standing at the exit door. Before any information is allowed out to the rest of the building (the Hidden State), the guard checks the whiteboard, decides what is relevant right now, and lets only those specific details pass through.

cards.title: The Mystery Novel Filter
cards.body: Imagine you are reading a long mystery book. The cell state remembers everything that has happened so far. When you want to guess who the culprit is right now, the Output Gate filters this massive history to highlight only the clues related to the suspect in the current chapter, ignoring the rest of the book's background details.

cards.title: Internal vs. External Memory
cards.body: People often think the hidden state is exactly the same as the cell state. In reality, the cell state is the complete internal memory, while the hidden state is just the filtered version of that memory that the Output Gate decides to show to the outside world!

cards.title: Visualizing the Gate
cards.body: See how the Output Gate acts as a screen between the internal memory and the outside world.

right.text: The filtered information that actually gets output.

left.text: Holds all long-term memories and facts internally.

exercises.prompt: What does the Output Gate directly help to calculate and produce?
exercises.explain: The output gate decides what information is sent out as the next hidden state, using the cell state as its source.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The Output Gate's main job is to overwrite and change the stored values inside the Cell State.
exercises.explain: The output gate does not change the stored cell state itself; it only controls what is read from it to create the hidden state.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how the output gate decides what to filter.
exercises.sentence: The output gate uses a [blank] function to decide which parts of the cell state to let through.
exercises.explain: The output gate uses a sigmoid function to create values between 0 and 1, which act as a filter on the cell state.

exercises.prompt: Which statement contains an error about how the Output Gate works?

options.text: The Output Gate uses a sigmoid filter to selectively output information from the cell state.
The Output Gate directly copies the raw input straight to the hidden state without any filtering.
The Output Gate regulates what information leaves the cell to influence the next step.

exercises.explain: The option mentioning raw input directly becoming the hidden state without gates is incorrect because it ignores the filtering process of the output gate.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following best describes the core job of the Output Gate in an LSTM?

options.text: It permanently deletes useless information from the cell state.
It filters the cell state to decide what to output as the next hidden state.
It decides how much new input to write into the cell state.
It acts as the primary memory storage area of the entire LSTM cell.

exercises.explain: The output gate acts as a filter. It takes the long-term memory (Cell State), squashes it to safe values, and uses its own sigmoid filter to decide which parts of that memory to reveal as the active short-term hidden state.
